ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03311737 covers milk tea as a general category. No specific code exists for low-fat caramel milk tea; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM covers tea-based beverages broadly. No specific code for caramel milk tea with low-fat milk; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 represents tea as a substance. No specific concept for caramel milk tea; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One medium cup of low fat caramel milk tea, approximately 500 ml as served at KOI Thé in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Milk Tea, with sugar — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented by USDA Branded Food data for low-fat caramel milk tea analogues
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for KOI Thé low-fat caramel milk tea. Values are estimated by combining USDA data for brewed black tea (FDC 171917), low-fat milk (FDC 172179), and caramel syrup (FDC 169640) in proportions consistent with a standard Taiwanese-style milk tea formulation (approximately 55% tea, 35% low-fat milk, 10% caramel syrup by volume). Energy and macros are cross-checked against USDA Branded Food entries for comparable commercial milk tea products. All values are per 100 g of the final beverage.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson et al.; milk tea and sweetened tea analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for KOI Thé low-fat caramel milk tea. GI of 55 is estimated based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened milk tea (GI ~55, which falls into the low category). The caramel syrup contributes sucrose-based sugars (GI ~65) and the low-fat milk contributes lactose (GI ~46); the blended GI of approximately 55 is consistent with published values for sweetened milk tea. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 51.0) / 100 = 28.1, using 500 g serving with 10.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (28 mg per 100 g); moderate sugar intake
hypertension.guidance: A medium serving (500 ml) contributes approximately 140 mg sodium, which is low and unlikely to significantly affect blood pressure, but the added caramel sugar content should be monitored in individuals managing hypertension alongside metabolic risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content from caramel syrup (9.8 g sugar per 100 g) and a low glycemic index (GI 55) but with a high glycemic load per serving (GL 28.1)
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the glycemic index (GI 55) is considered low, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid this beverage due to its high glycemic load per medium serving (GL ~28), driven by caramel syrup-derived sugars; requesting unsweetened or reduced-sugar versions and smaller portion sizes is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar from caramel syrup contributing to excess caloric intake; low saturated fat due to low-fat milk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low-fat milk base limits saturated fat exposure (0.4 g per 100 g), which is favourable for cardiovascular health, but the high added sugar content from caramel syrup may contribute to hypertriglyceridaemia and should be restricted in individuals with established cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Low Fat Caramel Milk Tea (Medium) is a Taiwanese-style sweetened milk tea beverage served at KOI Thé, a popular Taiwanese tea chain with outlets in Singapore. It is prepared by blending brewed black tea with low-fat milk and caramel syrup, resulting in a smooth, lightly creamy beverage with a sweet caramel flavour and mild tea bitterness. The low-fat milk formulation reduces the overall fat content compared to full-cream milk tea variants, making it a lighter option within the milk tea category. Per 100 g, the beverage provides approximately 52 kcal, 1.2 g protein, 0.6 g fat (of which 0.4 g is saturated), and 10.2 g carbohydrate (9.8 g sugars). A medium serving of approximately 500 ml delivers around 260 kcal and 51 g of carbohydrate, predominantly from added caramel syrup sugars. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low category), and the glycemic load per medium serving is approximately 28, which is clinically significant for individuals managing blood glucose. The beverage contains milk allergens and is not suitable for those with lactose intolerance or dairy allergies. It is vegetarian and generally considered halal. In Singapore, this drink is commonly consumed as an afternoon refreshment or social beverage. Clinically, the high sugar load from caramel syrup is the primary concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, or cardiovascular disease, and reduced-sugar or sugar-free alternatives should be recommended where available.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for KOI Thé low-fat caramel milk tea. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for brewed black tea, low-fat milk, and caramel syrup in proportions consistent with standard Taiwanese milk tea formulations, and cross-checked against USDA Branded Food entries for comparable commercial milk tea products. GI is estimated from the closest analogous beverage in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (brewed black tea FDC 171917, low-fat milk FDC 172179, caramel syrup FDC 169640) blended in approximate proportions for a standard Taiwanese-style milk tea. No exact product-level nutritional data was available from KOI Thé or an approved primary source. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero by formulation as no hydrogenated fats are used in this beverage category; confirmed zero per USDA component data. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solid ingredients; confirmed zero per USDA component data. 4. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 5. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 6. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 7. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened milk tea; no direct GI measurement for KOI Thé low-fat caramel milk tea was found. 8. Serving size of 500 g is an approximation for a medium cup as served at KOI Thé Singapore; actual volume may vary. 9. The food_id GD001359 is a field survey data seed record; this record is not present in the FD series food_index.json. 10. Multilingual names are provided for Taiwanese cuisine served in Singapore context, covering English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean multilingual naming rules. 11. All aliases_by_language entries are present verbatim in the flat aliases list. Hindi aliases use pure Devanagari script throughout: 'लो फैट कैरेमल मिल्क टी' and 'लो फैट कैरेमल मिल्क टी (मध्यम आकार)' appear identically in both aliases_by_language.hindi and the flat aliases array.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record with component-based nutrient estimation from USDA FoodData Central.
Iteration 2: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for glycemic_index 55. Attempted to fix Hindi alias consistency but introduced Tamil-script corruption in flat aliases Hindi entries.

Iteration 3: Root cause of q8 failure identified — flat aliases list contained Tamil-script characters ('லோ') in place of Devanagari ('लो') for Hindi entries, causing a character-level mismatch with aliases_by_language.hindi. Fixed by replacing corrupted flat aliases Hindi entries with pure Devanagari strings identical to those in aliases_by_language.hindi. Full character-by-character consistency audit performed across all language keys. All 17 flat alias entries now match their aliases_by_language counterparts verbatim.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
